review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to data integrity. However, the naming of the network policy function needed refinement for clarity and consistency. The other functions are well-named and accurately reflect the security controls they are intended to enforce.
naming_improvements: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_secure_transport_policy to k8s_networkpolicy_secure_transport_enabled to better indicate a pass/fail condition
title: 164_312_c_1: 164.312(c)(1) Integrity
step1_reasoning: The HIPAA control 164.312(c)(1) focuses on ensuring the integrity of electronic protected health information (ePHI). In a Kubernetes context, this can be mapped to ensuring that data at rest and in transit is protected against unauthorized modification. This involves enabling encryption for etcd (where cluster state and potentially sensitive information are stored) and Kubernetes Secrets to protect data integrity. Additionally, audit log integrity verification ensures that logs are not tampered with, and secure transport policies in NetworkPolicies ensure data integrity during transmission.
